Association: Genotype CT is associated with increased Drug Toxicity when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to genotype CC.
More Details: Grade >=2 lethargy, diarrhea, stomatitis and hand-foot syndrome. "Infection" was infection + grade >=3 neutropenia. No significant results were seen when considering any toxicity (p=0.13), neutropenia (n=0.06) or nausea + vomiting (p=0.19). Advanced colorectal cancer. n=834 received fluorouracil and n=1349 received capecitabine. All patients also received oxaliplatin. n=815 also received cetuximab. p-values were not corrected for multiple testing. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: DPYD deficiency is associated with increased risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil.
More Details: 19 patients with unanticipated severe fluorouracil-related toxicity and whose DPD activity score was <150 pmol/min/mg protein. The toxicity score (sum of WHO grading, theoretical range 0-20) was significantly higher in patients with markedly low DPD activity (n=11, below 100 pmol/min/mg protein) compared with patients with moderate DPD deficiency (n=8, 100 >= x < 150 pmol/min/mg protein). The mean toxicity score in those with markedly low DPD activity was 13.2+/-5.9 and in those with moderate DPD deficiency was 6.8+/-3.7.

Association: Allele T is associated with increased likelihood of Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil in people with Colonic Neoplasms or Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to allele C.
More Details: The PETACC-8 clinical trial included patients with colon cancer receiving FOLFOX4, with half receiving additional cetuximab. The cohort of the FFCD 2000-05 trial (patients with colorectal cancer) was used as a validation set. The T allele was associated with an increased risk of fluorouracil-related adverse events (overall toxicity) and hematological toxicity, as well as neutropenia. No significant results were seen for gastrointestinal toxicity. Additionally, no significant results were seen for mucositis (p=0.40), nausea (p=0.08) or diarrhea (p=0.08). Lastly, in the validation set, this allele was associated with an increased risk of overall toxicity (p=0.02) and hematological toxicity (p=0.002) but not gastrointestinal toxicity (p=0.5). Note that p=0.025 was considered significant for the replication analysis. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: DPYD poor metabolizer is associated with decreased dose of fluorouracil in people with Colorectal Neoplasms, Gastrointestinal Neoplasms and Rectal Neoplasms as compared to DPYD normal metabolizer.
More Details: The study was an attempt to verify a novel assay to phenotype DPYD enzyme activity. DPYD deficiency was based on monitoring endogenous dihydrouracil to uracil (UH2/U) ratio in plasma after standard liquid-liquid extraction using  HPLC-UV. Ratio values were used to determine DPD status as a continuous variable and because no mathematical model was available, patients were categorized on UH2/U ratio values into poor (mild, intermediate, profound) or extensive metabolizers. Doses were tailored to a patient's UH2/U ratio determined DPYD phenotype and after dose tailoring the authors found no significant difference between clinical benefit or toxicity between PM and EM groups.

All patients also received oxaliplatin. n=815 also received cetuximab. p-values were not corrected for multiple testing. From the discussion section:"Although no associations with common variants remained statistically significant after correction, it is noteworthy that the two common variants, most significantly associated with toxicity, were both in DPYD.". More Details: "The rs4294451 T-allele is relatively common, with a global minor allele frequency of 27% [16]. Our study is the first that we are aware of to find an association of rs4294451 T-allele count with lower 5-FU drug exposure (p = 0.049). The magnitude of the effect associated with the rs4294451 T-allele count appears to be clinically relevant; the 5-FU AUC for a heterozygous male carrier of this variant is predicted to be 17% lower than the 5-FU AUC for a male who is homozygous for the A/A genotype. Our finding of reduced 5-FU drug exposure in rs4294451 T-allele carriers is consistent with the prior finding that T-allele carriers had lower risk of toxicity with fluoropyrimidine-containing chemotherapy, as well as worse survival "

Association: Genotype CT is not associated with risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil in people with Neoplasms as compared to genotype TT.
More Details: Out of 76 patients with grade III or IV toxicity, there were 22 patients with the *1/*5 diplotype. Out of 48 patients with grade 0-I toxicity, there were 11 patients with the *1/*5 diplotype. Authors also state in the Discussion section that the frequency of the *5 allele did not significantly differ between high and low toxicity patients, but no information is provided on statistical analyses.

Association: Allele A is associated with decreased activity of DPYD when assayed with fluorouracil as compared to allele G.
More Details: Functional study. The A allele was associated with DPYD activity at 9% of wild-type. Activity measured by conversion of 5-fluorouracil to 5-dihydrofluorouracil, the product of DPYD catabolism. Using PolyPhen-2, this variant was classified as "probably damaging". Please note that this SNP was referred to by its amino acid change (R592W) within the paper. Alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Allele C is not associated with severity of Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil in people with Colonic Neoplasms and Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to allele T.
More Details: The PETACC-8 clinical trial included patients with colon cancer receiving FOLFOX4, with half receiving additional cetuximab. The cohort of the FFCD 2000-05 trial (patients with colorectal cancer) was used as a validation set. No significant association with grade>=3 fluorouracil-related adverse events (overall toxicity), gastrointestinal toxicity or hematological toxicity was seen in either trial, and no association with diarrhea, nausea, mucositis or neutropenia was seen in the PETACC-8 trial. For examples, information from the paper on overall toxicity in the two cohorts is provided; please refer to the Supplementary Information in the paper for all other results. Note that significance was set at p=0.004 for the PETACC cohort and p=0.025 for the FFCD cohort. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

More Details: There were 3 patients with the CT genotype (also known as *1/*2A) in the population with Grade 3-4 diarrhea, mucositis or neutropenia. One patient had their dose reduced by 50% in Cycle 1-2, the other by 25%. One patient withdrew from therapy during Cycle 3-4. When *2A, *4, *13 and the rs67376798 A allele were assessed together, there was a significantly increased risk for Grade 3-4 drug toxicity (p<1x10^-16). Variants were not statistically assessed alone. Please note alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Genotypes AG + GG is associated with increased concentrations of fluorouracil in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to genotype AA.
More Details: Patients with the *1/*5 or rs17376848 AG genotype (n=11) were classified as having partial DPYD deficiency. Patients with the *5/*5 or rs17376848 GG genotype (n=4) were classified as having complete DPYD deficiency. Those with partial deficiency had a median serum level of fluorouracil of 2.57 mcg/mL, those with complete deficiency had a median serum level of 11.51 mcg/mL, while those with the wild-type genotype for both variants (n=11) had a median serum level of 0.83 mcg/mL. It is unclear the proportion of the patients who had the *1/*5, *5/*5, AG or GG genotype.

Association: Genotype AA is associated with increased risk of Neurotoxicity Syndromes and Neutropenia when treated with fluorouracil, irinotecan or oxaliplatin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to genotypes AG + GG.
More Details: Patients were taking either IFL (irinotecan + fluorouracil + leucovorin; n=114), FOLFOX (fluorouracil + oxaliplatin + leucovorin; n=299) or IROX (irinotecan + oxaliplatin; n=107). Patients with the AA genotype who were taking FOLFOX had an increased risk for grade >=3 febrile neutropenia as compared to those with the AG or GG genotype. Patients with the AA genotype who were taking IROX had an increased likelihood of discontinuing treatment due to grade>=3 neurotoxicity as compared to those with the AG or GG genotype. No significant association with either of these toxicities was seen in patients taking IFL or considering all the treatment groups combined. No significant association with grade >=3 diarrhea or vomiting or grade >=4 neutropenia was seen in any of the treatment groups. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

The genotype-adjusted dosing group had a significantly decreased incidence of severe toxicities as compared to the reference group; one toxic death occurred in the reference group. There were no significant differences in efficacy between the groups.

Side effects were "bone marrow hypocellular", diarrhea and oral mucositis. Patients were treated with FOLFIRI regimen. DPYD activity was assessed using UH2/U (dihydrouracil/uracil) ratio. Patients were segregated into two categories using SN-38 levels: SN-38 levels at 1.5 hours after irinotecan infusion > 50.24 ng/ml and SN-38 levels at 49 hours after irinotecan infusion > 15.25 ng/ml. Within the first group, p-values were <0.001 for all three side effects. Within the second group, p-values were 0.005 for bone marrow hypocellular, 0.001 for diarrhea and 0.002 for mucositis.

Association: Allele T is associated with increased risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with capecitabine, fluorouracil or tegafur in people with Neoplasms.
More Details: Meta-analysis with 6 studies. The T allele was associated with a significantly increased risk of grade >=3 overall toxicity, gastrointestinal toxicity and hematological toxicity. No significant results were seen for hand-foot syndrome (p=0.65). Note that the meta-analysis included one unpublished study by the authors. The effect of the T allele on risk of toxicity seemed similar for studies assessing a long timeframe vs a short timeframe. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Genotypes CC + CT is not associated with Drug Toxicity when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to genotype TT.
More Details: Grade >=2 any toxicity (p=0.27), neutropenia (p=0.08), lethargy (p=0.63), nausea + vomiting (p=0.81), diarrhea (p=0.85), stomatitis (p=0.37), hand-foot syndrome (p=0.83) or infection (p=0.97). Advanced colorectal cancer. n=834 received fluorouracil and n=1349 received capecitabine. All patients also received oxaliplatin. n=815 also received cetuximab. p-values were not corrected for multiple testing. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Allele A is associated with increased severity of Drug Toxicity when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil as compared to allele G.
More Details: Alleles complemented. "A total of 24 non-synonymous, frameshift, and splice site variants were detected in 986 individuals (excluding patients carrying any of the four known variants (DPYD*2A, c.1236G > A, c.2846A > T, and c.1679 T > G) tested for in the Alpe-DPD study.""In total, seven rare deleterious variants were identified, which were carried by 10 individuals. Five variants in the coding region (c.1670C > T, c.1913 T > C, c.1925 T > C, c.506delC, and c.731A > C) and two variants in the flanking splice region (c.1740 + 1G > T and c.763 − 2A > G) were predicted to be deleterious." Variant not observed in the group with severe toxicity.

Association: Genotype CT is associated with decreased clearance of fluorouracil in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to genotype CC.
More Details: 5-fluorouracil activity and DPD activity were compared between one patient with severe fluorouracil-induced toxicity and six control patients with normal 5-fluorouracil-related symptoms. All patients were being treated for colorectal cancer. The index patient was a 60-year-old white female who experienced leukopenia, thrombocytopenia, nausea, diarrhea, stomatitis, fever, hair loss. The control patients experienced mild nausea, vomiting, or grade 1 stomatitis. The clearance was 520 ml/min in the index patient vs 980-1780 mg/min in controls. The area under the curve from 0-3 hours in the index patient was 24.1 mgh/l vs 15.3 mgh/l as the highest value in controls. Sequence analysis revealed the index patient was heterozygous for the *2A (IVS14+1G>A) mutation. Sequence analysis of the six control patients revealed no mutations.

Association: Genotype AA is not associated with response to fluorouracil, irinotecan or oxaliplatin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to genotypes AG + GG.
More Details: Patients were taking either IFL (irinotecan + fluorouracil + leucovorin; n=114), FOLFOX (fluorouracil + oxaliplatin + leucovorin; n=299) or IROX (irinotecan + oxaliplatin; n=107). No significant association was seen between this variant and confirmed response rate in any of the treatment groups OR all treatment groups considered together. Significance level was set at 0.01. rs10929302 is reported as UGT1A1*93 (UGT1A1 *93/*93 is not associated with response to fluorouracil, irinotecan or oxaliplatin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to UGT1A1 *1/*1 + *1/*93).

Association: DPYD deficiency is associated with risk of Neurotoxicity Syndromes when treated with fluorouracil in women with Breast Neoplasms.
More Details: Case report. This patient was later genotyped in PMID 11895907. A 40-year-old white woman was diagnosed with breast cancer. She was treated with fluorouracil, cyclophosphamide and methotrexate. On the first cycle (fluorouracil dose = 1100 mg), day 11, she was hospitalized with neutropenia, defervesced on antibiotics and was sent home without complaints. On the second cycle (dose = 600 mg), day 14, she was found to have a white blood cell count of 1300. On the third cycle (dose = 400 mg), day 14, she was hospitalized with fever, neutropenia and "clumsy gait". She developed neurotoxicity (unable to walk, speak), which she recovered from over a period of months. she developed neurotoxicity. She was found to have markedly prolonged elimination half-life of fluorouracil (159 min), and no evidence of fluorouracil catabolites were seen in plasma or cerebrospinal fluid. She was found to have complete deficiency of DPYD enzyme activity in peripheral blood mononuclear cells. Her father and children had partial deficiency.

However, 39/51 of patients with this allele developed severe toxicity. The impact of the allele was higher in studies in which the incidence of severe toxicity was low. Alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand (reported as IVS14+1G>A in the paper). Studies included patients treated with capecitabine alone or in a combination therapy, tegafur-uracil, 5-FU alone or a 5-FU-based regimen, 5-FU or capecitabine.

After the first cycle, she developed grade 3 neutropenia, grade 3 mucositis and grade 2 diarrhea. DPYD was tested for *2A, *2B, *3, *7, *8, *9B, *10, *11, *12, *13, rs67376798 and rs2297595. Association: Genotype AG is associated with risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil in women with Colonic Neoplasms.
More Details: Case report. A 76-year-old woman began chemotherapy after radical hemicolectomy for a T3N2 colon cancer. 5-fluorouracil and calcium folinate. She developed grade 3-4 oral and intestinal mucositis and grades 3-4 diarrhea. Treatment reintroduced at a 50% dose reduction, which was tolerated with a minor degree of toxicity. DPYD was sequenced and patient was found to be heterozygous for this SNP (c.1796T>C, M599T). Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Genotypes AG + GG is associated with increased Infection, nephrotoxicity and Toxic liver disease when treated with fluorouracil in children with Precursor Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia-Lymphoma as compared to genotype AA.
More Details: The proportion of liver function damage (p=0.012), nephrotoxicity (p=0.043) and infection rate (p=0.034) was higher in patients with the AG or GG genotype (*1/*9A or *9A/*9A) as compared to patients with the AA genotype (*1/*1). No significant results were seen for mucosal lesion (p=0.926), gastrointestinal reaction (p=0.8) or cytopenia (p=0.831). Alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Genotype CT is associated with increased risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil in women with Pancreatic Neoplasms as compared to genotype CC.
More Details: Case report. A 59-year-old woman was treated with FOLFIRINOX (irinotecan, 5-fluorouracil, leucovorin and oxaliplatin) for metastatic pancreatic adenocarcinoma. She developed grade 4 mucositis, grade 1 diarrhea, febrile neutropenia and an erythematous rash with desquamation. Owing to these side effects, FOLFIRINOX was discontinued. The patients was tested for DPYD and UGT1A1 variants. She was heterozygous for the DPYD*2A variant. She was also heterozygous for DPYD*4 and DPYD*6 variants. She also had UGT1A1 deficiency.

Association: Allele G is associated with decreased response to fluorouracil, Platinum compounds and radiotherapy in people with Stomach Neoplasms as compared to allele A.
More Details: 1,002 patients with gastric cancer and complete genotyping data were included; 694 had chemotherapy treatment, and 308 did not. Median follow-up time was 63.43 months, with 465 deaths (46.4%) at the end. Patients were treated with fluoropyridines, platinum-based chemotherapy, or radiotherapy combinations.  In multivariate analysis the number of risk alleles was significantly associated with survival (1-3 alleles HR 1.66 (95% CI 1.06-2.59) 3-6 alleles HR 2.28 (95% CI 1.28-4.03) P-value for trend = 0.001). Please note: the authors do not distinguish between distinct treatment regimens- simply "chemotherapy".

Association: Genotype (CCGCGCCACTTGGCCTGCCTCCGTCCCG)2/(CCGCGCCACTTGGCCTGCCTCCGTCCCG)3 is associated with increased survival when treated with cisplatin and fluorouracil in people with Stomach Neoplasms.
More Details:
Case reports of 2 "patients experienced prolonged survival and benefited significantly from repeated 5-FU/cisplatin combination
chemotherapy"

Association: Genotype CT is associated with Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil in people with Neoplasms.
More Details: Only 2 heterozygotes for DPYD IVS14+1G>A were found within a cohort of patients with documented fluorouracil toxicity (at least grade 3 toxicity or grade 2 neurotoxicity). Both patients had low DPYD activity (44 and 142 pmol/min/mg). However, these were not among the 4 individuals who experienced fatal toxicity and were tested for this SNP. Additionally, DPYD activity in patients with 5-fluorouracil toxicity was significantly lower than that of the general cancer population (p=0.02).

Association: DPYD deficiency is associated with increased risk of Neutropenia when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Neoplasms.
More Details: 58% of patients with low DPD activity suffered from grade IV neutropenia, compared with 29% of patients with normal DPD activity. "Normal" activity was defined as >70% that of the normal population, while low activity was <70% of the normal population. Note that the proportion of females in the group with grade IV neutropenia was significantly higher than that of males (72% vs 28%, p=0.03). The cohort used in this study may overlap with that used in PMID 12360106.

Association: Allele A is associated with increased response to fluorouracil, Platinum compounds or radiotherapy in people with Stomach Neoplasms as compared to allele C.
More Details: 1,002 patients with gastric cancer and complete genotyping data were included; 694 had chemotherapy treatment, and 308 did not. Median follow-up time was 63.43 months, with 465 deaths (46.4%) at the end. Patients were treated with fluoropyridines, platinum-based chemotherapy, or radiotherapy combinations.  In multivariate analysis the number of risk alleles was significantly associated with survival (1-3 alleles HR 1.66 (95% CI 1.06-2.59) 3-6 alleles HR 2.28 (95% CI 1.28-4.03) P-value for trend = 0.001). Please note: the authors do not distinguish between distinct treatment regimens- simply "chemotherapy".

Association: Genotype GG is associated with decreased response to bevacizumab, fluorouracil, irinotecan and leucovorin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to genotypes AA + AG.
More Details: Patients were receiving treatment with bevacizumab + FOLFIRI (fluorouracil, leucovorin, irinotecan). Patients with the GG genotype had a significantly lower tumor response rate according to the RECIST criteria. However, when the p-value was adjusted for multiple testing, it was no longer significant (p=0.63). When considering only tumors on the left-sided colon and rectum, this SNP was also associated with tumor response rate.

Association: Genotype CT is associated with increased Infection and Toxic liver disease when treated with fluorouracil in children with Precursor Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia-Lymphoma as compared to genotype CC.
More Details: The proportion of liver function damage (p=0.026) and infection rate (p=0.03) was higher in patients with the CT genotype as compared to patients with the CC genotype. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Genotype (CCGCGCCACTTGGCCTGCCTCCGTCCCG)2/(CCGCGCCACTTGGCCTGCCTCCGTCCCG)3 is associated with risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil in women with Rectal Neoplasms.
More Details: Case report. A 56-year-old woman was diagnosed with rectal cancer. She was started on chemoradiation which included fluorouracil. Within 24 hours of treatment, she developed severe diarrhea and nausea, then oropharyngeal mucositis, severe neutropenia and thrombocytopenia and was hospitalized. DPYD and TMYS genes were tested. Meanwhile, she was treated with uridine triacetate for the fluorouracil toxicity. She was found to have the TYMS 2R/3RC mutation; no DPYD polymorphisms were found. Please note that the 3RC mutation represents a G>C change at rs2853542 in addition to the 3 repeats at rs45445694.

Association: Allele C is not associated with Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to allele T.
More Details: Risk allele not specified in article, assumed minor allele as risk allele and minor allele estimated from dbSNP. This SNP was used as a proxy for rs1801019 in an attempt to replicate published association.

Association: Allele C is not associated with severity of gastrointestinal toxicity when treated with fluorouracil, irinotecan and leucovorin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to allele T.
More Details: The study comprised overall more than 400 metastatic colorectal cancer (mCRC) patients treated with first-line FOLFIRI (folinic acid/leucovorin, fluorocil, irinotecan) grouped in a discovery and a replication cohorts. The variant was not associated with severity of gastrointestinal toxicity (grade 3-4).

Association: Allele C is not associated with severity of gastrointestinal toxicity when treated with fluorouracil, irinotecan and leucovorin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to allele A.
More Details: The study comprised overall more than 400 metastatic colorectal cancer (mCRC) patients treated with first-line FOLFIRI (folinic acid/leucovorin, fluorocil, irinotecan) grouped in a discovery and a replication cohorts. The variant was not associated with severity of gastrointestinal toxicity (grade 3-4).

Association: Genotypes CT + TT is associated with risk of Neutropenia when treated with fluorouracil in people with Neoplasms.
More Details: Analysis of the DPYD gene for presence of DPYD*2A (IVS14+1G>A) in patients suffering from grade IV neutropenia found that 50% of patients were heterozygous or homozygous for this mutation. This mutation was detected in 1 patient with normal DPYD activity (just above the upper threshold risk level). Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.  The cohort used in this study may overlap with that used in PMID 12360106.

Association: Allele A is not associated with Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil and oxaliplatin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to allele G.
More Details: Risk allele not specified in article, assumed minor allele as risk allele and minor allele estimated from dbSNP. This SNP was used as a proxy for rs1695 in an attempt to replicate published association.

Association: Genotype AC is associated with increased risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil in women with Breast Neoplasms as compared to genotype CC.
More Details: Case report. A patient with decreased DPD activity accompanied by severe 5-fluorouracil toxicity was first reported in a different paper (see original article for citation). She was a 57-year-old woman with breast cancer, who presented with leukopenia, thrombocytopenia and severe mucositis during 5-fluorouracil therapy. Her DPD activity in lymphocytes was 11 pmol/min/mg protein (normal control = 258.2 +/- 144.8 pmol/min/mg protein). Analysis of the DPD gene showed she carried the DPYD*11 mutation on one allele (1003G>T, V335L) and the *12 mutation on the other (62G>A + 1156G>T, R21Q + E386Ter). Relative exogenous DPD activity as measured in E.coli lysates was 35.6 +/- 35.0 pmol/min/mg protein for *11, not detectable for *12, 202.5 +/- 60.2 for R21Q, and not detectable for E386Ter; enzymatic activity of wild-type DPD was 209.0 +/- 127.2.

Association: Genotypes CT + TT is associated with increased risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Neoplasms as compared to genotype CC.
More Details: Presence of the CT or TT genotypes was more common in patients who developed Grade >=3 toxicity as compared to those who only developed Grade <3 toxicity. Significant associations with hematological toxicities and neutropenia were also seen, though no association with intestinal toxicity was found. Authors also note that the variant was associated with shorter median time to toxicity (TTT) as compared to wild-type (p=0.0022). Patients had various types of cancer and many were treated with additional chemotherapy drugs besides fluorouracil or capecitabine. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Allele T is associated with decreased catalytic activity of DPYD when treated with fluorouracil in 293FT cells as compared to allele G.
More Details: One of 21 variants found in 1020 individuals in Japan. Assays were down in triplicate.

Association: Allele C is not associated with severity of gastrointestinal toxicity when treated with fluorouracil, irinotecan and leucovorin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to allele A.
More Details: The study comprised overall more than 400 metastatic colorectal cancer (mCRC) patients treated with first-line FOLFIRI (folinic acid/leucovorin, fluorocil, irinotecan) grouped in a discovery and a replication cohorts. The variant was not associated with severity of gastrointestinal toxicity (grade 3-4).

Association: Genotype AT is associated with increased exposure to fluorouracil in women with Colonic Neoplasms.
More Details: Case report. A 49-year-old woman was treated with FOLFOX and developed grade IV mucositis, diarrhea and thrombocytopenia, as well as grade III alopecia. Six months later she complained from cognitive decline. Analysis showed strongly decreased DPYD activity. Sequence analysis revealed that the patient was a heterozygote at rs67376798. Also had a novel nonsense mutation (c1681C>T -p.R561X) that results in no residual DPD activity.

Association: Genotype AT is associated with increased severity of Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil and leucovorin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to genotype TT.
More Details: 8 individuals were heterozygous for this variant, one of whom also was heterozyous for both *2A (rs3918290) and 85T>C (rs1801265). Please note alleles complemented to reflect the genotype on the positive chromosomal strand.

Association: Genotype CC is not associated with Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil in children with Precursor Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia-Lymphoma as compared to genotype CT.
More Details: No significant differences in the mucosal lesion, gastrointestinal reaction, liver function damage, renal function damage, cytopenia or infection were observed in between the genotypes. Alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Allele C is associated with decreased catalytic activity of DPYD when treated with fluorouracil in 293FT cells as compared to allele T.
More Details: One of 21 variants found in 1020 individuals in Japan. Assays were down in triplicate.

Association: Genotype (CCGCGCCACTTGGCCTGCCTCCGTCCCG)3/(CCGCGCCACTTGGCCTGCCTCCGTCCCG)3 is associated with increased expression of TYMS when treated with fluorouracil in colonic mucosa of colorectal cancer patients as compared to genotypes (CCGCGCCACTTGGCCTGCCTCCGTCCCG)2/(CCGCGCCACTTGGCCTGCCTCCGTCCCG)2 + (CCGCGCCACTTGGCCTGCCTCCGTCCCG)2/(CCGCGCCACTTGGCCTGCCTCCGTCCCG)3.
More Details: compared to the 2R/2R-2R/3RC-2R/3RG genotypes (3RC has the additional SNP in the repeat).

Association: Genotype AT is associated with decreased clearance of fluorouracil in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to genotype TT.
More Details: 8 individuals were heterozygous for this variant, one of whom also was heterozygous for both *2A (rs3918290) and 85T>C (rs1801265). Please note alleles complemented to reflect the genotype on the positive chromosomal strand.

Association: Genotypes AA + AT is associated with increased risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Neoplasms as compared to genotype TT.
More Details: Presence of the AA or AT genotypes was more common in patients who developed Grade >=3 toxicity as compared to those who only developed Grade <3 toxicity. A significant associations with neutropenia was also seen. Authors also note that the variant was associated with shorter median time to toxicity (TTT) as compared to wild-type (p<0.0001). Patients had various types of cancer and many were treated with additional chemotherapy drugs besides fluorouracil or capecitabine. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Genotype AA is not associated with risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Neoplasms as compared to genotypes AC + CC.
More Details: Not significant for either global toxicity, gastrointestinal toxicity or hospitalization. The variant was associated with increased risk of hematological toxicity (p=0.018). The authors did find that uracil concentrations were associated with toxicity; DPYD is responsible for the conversion of uracil to dihydrouracil. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Genotype CC is associated with increased response to fluorouracil in children with Precursor Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia-Lymphoma as compared to genotype CT.
More Details: Patients with the CC genotype had a higher complete remission (CR) rate as compared to those with the CT genotype. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Allele G is not associated with severity of gastrointestinal toxicity when treated with fluorouracil, irinotecan and leucovorin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to allele A.
More Details: The study comprised overall more than 400 metastatic colorectal cancer (mCRC) patients treated with first-line FOLFIRI (folinic acid/leucovorin, fluorocil, irinotecan) grouped in a discovery and a replication cohorts. The variant was not associated with severity of gastrointestinal toxicity (grade 3-4).

Association: Allele C is associated with decreased catalytic activity of DPYD when assayed with fluorouracil in 293FT cells as compared to allele T.
More Details: One of 21 variants found in 1020 individuals in Japan. Assays were down in triplicate.

Association: DPYD deficiency is not associated with increased severity of Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil in people with Keratosis or Warts as compared to DPYD non-deficient.
Biogeographical Groups: Multiple groups, 97.5% Caucasian, 1% Asian, 0.5% Black, 0.5% American Indian/Pacific Islander, 05.% Unknown/Not Reported
More Details:
with TOPICAL 5-FU. "Patients carrying
any DPYD variant had a nominally higher rate of grade 1+ toxicity than DPYD wild-type patients,
however, this did not reach statistical significance (21.4% [3/14] vs. 10.2% [19/187], odds ratio
(OR)=2.40, 95% Confidence Interval: 0.10-2.53, p=0.19)." "The current analysis focused on carriers of the five DPYD
alleles (DPYD*2A (rs3918290), DPYD*13 (rs55886062), DPYD p.D949V (rs67376798) DPYD HapB3
(rs56038477) and DPYD p.Y186C (rs115232898))" "No patients carried two variant alleles or experienced grade 3+ toxicity."

Association: Genotype AG is associated with decreased progression-free survival when treated with bevacizumab, fluorouracil, irinotecan and leucovorin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to genotype GG.
More Details: Patients were receiving treatment with bevacizumab + FOLFIRI (fluorouracil, leucovorin, irinotecan). Patients with the AG genotype had a median progression-free survival (PFS) time of 10 months as compared to patients with the GG genotype who had a median PFS of 11 months. This SNP was only significant in univariate analysis, not in multivariate analysis (p=0.18). When considering only tumors on the left-sided colon and rectum, this SNP was also associated with median PFS, but again only in univariate analysis. Please note alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Allele A is associated with risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Neoplasms.
More Details: Patients with severe fluoropyrimidine-related early toxicity (at least one episode = grade >= 3) were referred for determination of DPD deficiency. 4 of 47 patients carried this variant (2846A>T or D949V). Toxicities were grade 3 or 4 diarrhea (n=2), neutropenia (n=1) or mucositis (n=1). Note that this allele was referred to as "DPYD 9B" at one point in the paper. However, based on the nucleotide and amino acid changes, this allele is now referred to as rs67376798, while DPYD*9B is made up of different variants (see DPYD haplotype sheet on PharmGKB). This allele was detected in patients with both low and normal DPD activity; this may have to do with the DPD enzyme assay. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Genotype CT is associated with increased severity of Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to genotype CC.
More Details: 3 individuals were heterozygous for this variant, one of whom also was heterozygous for both 2846A>T (rs67376798) and 85T>C (rs1801265).

Association: Allele A is not associated with severity of gastrointestinal toxicity when treated with fluorouracil, irinotecan and leucovorin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to allele G.
More Details: The study comprised overall more than 400 metastatic colorectal cancer (mCRC) patients treated with first-line FOLFIRI (folinic acid/leucovorin, fluorocil, irinotecan) grouped in a discovery and a replication cohorts. The variant was not associated with severity of gastrointestinal toxicity (grade 3-4).

Association: Genotypes AC + CC is not associated with risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Neoplasms as compared to genotype AA.
More Details: No significant difference in presence of the AC or CC genotypes was seen between patients who developed Grade >=3 toxicity and those who only developed Grade <3 toxicity. However, the authors suggest that this is due to small sample size. 2 patients with the AC or CC genotype developed Grade >=3 toxicity; 0 patients with the AC or CC genotype developed Grade <3 toxicity. Authors note that the variant was associated with shorter median time to toxicity (TTT) as compared to wild-type (p=0.02). Patients had various types of cancer and many were treated with additional chemotherapy drugs besides fluorouracil or capecitabine. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Allele A is associated with decreased catalytic activity of DPYD when assayed with fluorouracil in 293FT cells as compared to allele C.
More Details: One of 21 variants found in 1020 individuals in Japan. Assays were down in triplicate. Note the variant is translated as V335M in the article however the C>A change results in V335L.

Association: Genotype AA is associated with increased response to fluorouracil in children with Precursor Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia-Lymphoma as compared to genotype GG.
More Details: Patients with the AA genotype had a higher complete remission (CR) rate as compared to those with the GG genotype. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Allele T is associated with increased likelihood of Drug Toxicity or Death when treated with fluorouracil as compared to allele C.
More Details: "The patient initially showed signs of fluorouracil-related GI toxicity on day 8, followed by neutropenia on day 11. Attempted treatment to reverse the severe neutropenia was unsuccessful, ultimately resulting in multiorgan failure and patient death." "Postmortem genetic testing indicated the presence of four DPYD exonic variants: c.85T>C (rs1801265, p.C29R), c.496A>G (rs2297595), c.704G>A (rs755416212, p.R235Q), and c.2194G>A (rs1801160, p.V732I; Table 2). Unfortunately, preserved patient specimens were found to be unsuitable for assessing ex vivo DPD function within peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs) or for measuring plasma uracil and/or dihydrouracil levels. The c.704G>A variant is suspected as the primary contributor to the observed toxicities and presumed DPD deficiency. "

Association: Genotype CT is associated with decreased clearance of fluorouracil in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to genotype CC.
More Details: 3 individuals were heterozygous for this variant, one of whom also was heterozygous for both 2846A>T (rs67376798) and 85T>C (rs1801265).

Association: Allele G is not associated with severity of Neurotoxicity Syndromes when treated with fluorouracil, leucovorin and oxaliplatin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to allele A.

Genotype (CCGCGCCACTTGGCCTGCCTCCGTCCCG)2/(CCGCGCCACTTGGCCTGCCTCCGTCCCG)2 is not associated with risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Neoplasms as compared to genotypes (CCGCGCCACTTGGCCTGCCTCCGTCCCG)2/(CCGCGCCACTTGGCCTGCCTCCGTCCCG)3 + (CCGCGCCACTTGGCCTGCCTCCGTCCCG)3/(CCGCGCCACTTGGCCTGCCTCCGTCCCG)3.
Biogeographical Groups: Multiple groups, Caucasian, Afro-Caribbean, South Asian, South East Asian
More Details: No significant differences in occurrence of grade 0-2 or 3-4 toxicities (diarrhea, mucositis and neutropenia) were seen between any of the genotypes 2R/2R, 2R/3R or 3R/3R. Toxicity grade was classified according to the National Cancer Institute Common Toxicity Criteria version 3. Patients were receiving fluorouracil- or capecitabine-based chemotherapy.

Association: Allele A is not associated with response to fluorouracil, Platinum compounds and radiotherapy in people with Stomach Neoplasms as compared to allele G.
More Details: 1,002 patients with gastric cancer and complete genotyping data were included; 694 had chemotherapy treatment, and 308 did not. Median follow-up time was 63.43 months, with 465 deaths (46.4%) at the end. Patients were treated with fluoropyridines, platinum-based chemotherapy, or radiotherapy combinations. Please note: the authors do not distinguish between distinct treatment regimens- simply "chemotherapy".

Association: Allele G is not associated with activity of DPYD when assayed with fluorouracil as compared to allele C.
More Details: Functional study. The G allele was associated with DPYD activity at 117% of wild-type, a non-significant difference. Activity measured by conversion of 5-fluorouracil to 5-dihydrofluorouracil, the product of DPYD catabolism. Using PolyPhen-2, this variant was classified as "benign". Please note that this SNP was referred to by its amino acid change (V1021L) within the paper.

Association: Allele T is associated with increased risk of Neutropenia due to cyclophosphamide, epirubicin and fluorouracil in women with Breast Neoplasms as compared to allele C.
More Details: Association is designated as nominal as correction for multiple comparisons was not carried out.

Association: Genotypes AG + GG is not associated with risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Colonic Neoplasms as compared to genotype AA.
More Details: No association was seen between this variant and time-to-toxicity. Toxicity included grade >=3 neutropenia, diarrhea, asthenia, nausea, vomiting, leukopenia, thrombocytopenia, mucositis, stomatitis and skin toxicity. No association was seen with time-to-toxicity for specifically neutropenia. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Genotype CT is associated with increased likelihood of Drug Toxicity or Discontinuation when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Colorectal Neoplasms, Breast Neoplasms or Neoplasms as compared to genotype CC.
More Details: Alleles complemented to plus chromosomal strand. "Nine DPYD variants were identified with a higher frequency in the toxicity group (c.85C>T, c.150+1G>A, c.218T>G, c.515G>A, c.775A>G, c.1218G>A, c.1627A>G, c.1863G>A, and c.2194G>A) than in the control group. " c.150+1G>A maps to rs375356630 shown in table 4 as rs37535663, confirmed correct rs number with authors. "The DPYD gene variant c.150+1G>A variant in the DPYD gene is a splicing mutation"

Association: Allele G is associated with increased risk of Neutropenia due to cyclophosphamide, epirubicin and fluorouracil in women with Breast Neoplasms as compared to allele C.
More Details: Association is designated as nominal as correction for multiple comparisons was not carried out.

Association: Allele T is not associated with increased severity of Neurotoxicity Syndromes when treated with fluorouracil, leucovorin and oxaliplatin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to allele C.
Genotype CT is associated with increased severity of Drug Toxicity when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Neoplasms.
More Details: The genotypes were identified prior to treatment and treatment was adjusted according to the Dutch Pharmacogenetics Working Group guidelines. Dose reductions given for 3 out of 4 patients with the variant. These patients still experienced grades 3 and 4 toxicity even despite lower doses. The 4th patient had additional variants and did not receive chemotherapy.

Association: Genotype AT is not associated with risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Colonic Neoplasms as compared to genotype TT.

Toxicity included grade >=3 neutropenia, diarrhea, asthenia, nausea, vomiting, leukopenia, thrombocytopenia, mucositis, stomatitis and skin toxicity. No association was seen with time-to-toxicity for specifically neutropenia. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Genotype CC is associated with better survival outcome when treated with fluorouracil in people with Stomach Neoplasms as compared to genotype GG.
More Details: Please note; these are the alleles stated in the journal, however the gene is on the minus strand, and for other variants reported the alleles given are on the plus strand or minus strand, therefore it is not clear if those given for this variant are on the plus strand.This variant is in LD with SNPs rs3110697, rs2854744 and rs2960436, and the haplotype associated with the best survival outcome contained the C allele of this variant. Patients were treated with palliative combination chemotherapy.

Association: Allele A is not associated with risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Neoplasms as compared to allele G.
Biogeographical Groups: Multiple groups, Caucasian, Afro-Caribbean, South Asian, South East Asian
More Details: No significant differences in occurrence of grade 0-2 or 3-4 toxicities (diarrhea, mucositis and neutropenia) were seen between any of the genotypes. Toxicity grade was classified according to the National Cancer Institute Common Toxicity Criteria version 3. Patients were receiving fluorouracil- or capecitabine-based chemotherapy.

Association: Allele A is associated with decreased activity of DPYD when assayed with fluorouracil as compared to allele C.
More Details: "Eight variants showed modest reductions that were 15–35% of wildtype activity (M77V, C87F, Q156R, I560N, T573I, R692W, G767V, and S994T)." G767V maps to rs568367673C>A in table S2.

Association: Allele C is not associated with response to fluorouracil, Platinum compounds and radiotherapy in people with Stomach Neoplasms as compared to allele T.
More Details: 1,002 patients with gastric cancer and complete genotyping data were included; 694 had chemotherapy treatment, and 308 did not. Median follow-up time was 63.43 months, with 465 deaths (46.4%) at the end. Patients were treated with fluoropyridines, platinum-based chemotherapy, or radiotherapy combinations. Please note: the authors do not distinguish between distinct treatment regimens- simply "chemotherapy".

Association: Genotype AT is associated with increased severity of Drug Toxicity when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Neoplasms as compared to genotype TT.
More Details: Fluoropyrimidine-based chemotherapy. Grade >= 3 toxicity of any type. The most common toxicities were neutropenia and diarrhea, followed by leukopenia, stomatitis and nausea/vomiting. No significant results were seen when considering hematological toxicity or non-hematological toxicities on their own. p-value adjusted using Bonferroni and bootstrap corrections. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Allele G is not associated with severity of Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil.
More Details: No significant difference in the frequency of the G allele was seen between patients with grade 0-2 toxicity ("controls") and patients with grade 3-4 toxicity ("cases"). Allele referred to in the paper as 85T>C. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the positive strand.

Association: Genotype TT is associated with increased severity of Neurotoxicity Syndromes when treated with fluorouracil, leucovorin and oxaliplatin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to genotypes CC + CT.
More Details: Authors considered FDR adjusted p value less than 0.1 as significant. Adjusted p value (q value) shown in study parameters.

Association: Allele A is not associated with activity of DPYD when assayed with fluorouracil as compared to allele G.
More Details: Functional study. The A allele was associated with DPYD activity at 99% of wild-type, a non-significant difference. More Details: Case report. This patient was later genotyped in PMID 11895907. A 40-year-old white woman was diagnosed with breast cancer. She was treated with fluorouracil and developed neurotoxicity and neutropenia. She was found to have markedly prolonged elimination half-life of fluorouracil (159 min), decreased clearance (70 ml/min/m2) and no evidence of fluorouracil catabolites were seen in plasma or cerebrospinal fluid. She was found to have complete deficiency of DPYD enzyme activity in peripheral blood mononuclear cells. Her father and children had partial deficiency.

Sequence analysis of the genomic region encompassing the skipped exon showed that the patient was heterozygous for a G to A point mutation in the invariant GT splice donor sequence in the intron downstream of the skipped exon. This variant is now referred to as DPYD*2A. DPD activity in leukocytes (3.1 nmol/mg/h) was decreased compared to mean activity of DPD in controls (9.6+/-3.7), and comparable to that observed for obligate heterozygotes.

Association: Allele C is associated with increased risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Neoplasms.
More Details: Presence of the C allele was associated with severe (grade >=3) toxicity, specifically hematologic toxicity and dehydration. One patient with the CC genotype suffered lethal toxicity, and a compound heterozygote with the C allele for this SNP and the C allele for rs55886062 suffered life-threatening (grade 4) toxicity. The C allele was also associated with early fluoropyrimidine dose intervention. Additionally, when combining this variant with rs67376798 A, rs3918290 T, and rs55886062 C as "risk variant carriers", this combined group had a significantly increased risk of grade >=3 toxicity and significantly increased risk of dose intervention. Additionally, 2 carriers of this allele were found in 15 additional patients, 14 with Grade III-V toxicity and one with Grade II toxicity. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

One patient with the C allele had grade 3-5 toxicity and one had grade 2 toxicity. Both had a dose intervention. However, when combining this variant with rs67376798A, rs3918290 T, and rs75017182 C as "risk variant carriers", this combined group had a significantly increased risk of grade >=3 toxicity and significantly increased risk of dose intervention. Additionally, 2 carriers of this allele were found in a group of 15 individuals, 14 with Grade III-V toxicity and one with Grade II toxicity. She developed grade 4 neutropenia, grade 3 mucositis and grade 2 alopecia 10 days after the first cycle. She was found to have partial deficit of DPYD activity, estimated by a reduced dihydrouracil/uracil ratio (5.2 compared with 19 for median laboratory value). Sequencing revealed the DPYD*9A allele, the DPYD*5 allele and this variant - rs115232898 or Y186C. 5-fluorouracil dose was reduced. The authors state that *9A and *5 are "common polymorphisms without functional consequence" and that their paper "highlights the greater risk of developing...toxicity for p.Y186C heterozygous patients".

Association: Genotype AG is associated with increased risk of Cardiomyopathies when treated with fluorouracil in people with Rectal Neoplasms.
More Details: Case report. Variant referred to in the paper as c.85T>C. A 35-year-old man with stage IIIB rectal adenocarcinoma received 5-fluorouracil concurrent with pelvic radiation followed by surgery with colostomy. The patient was then started on mFOLFOX-6 but was admitted the same evening due to cardiomyopathy. FOLFOX was discontinued and IROX was started. Follow-up showed complete cardiac function recovery. The patient was found to be heterozygous for the c.85T>C variant, "resulting in reduced DPYD enzymatic activity and potentially increased risk of 5-FU toxicity" according to the authors. However, it should be noted that the patient also carried the TYMS 2R/2R genotype which predicts low TYMS expression, which the authors state "also increases the risk for 5-FU toxicity" and that the c.85T>C variant has been assigned a normal function by CPIC. The patient's brother died while receiving 5-FU-containing chemotherapy for advanced gastric cancer.

In multivariate analysis, this SNP was the worst prognostic factor, conferring the highest risk of disease recurrence (other factors included sex, tumor site, tumor stage). Please note that a significant interaction was found between tumor stage and this SNP: 73% of those with the GT or TT genotype had stage III disease vs. 100% with the GG genotype (p=0.0046). Alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

After the second cycle, grade 2 stomatitis appeared, and after the fourth cycle grade 4 diarrhea led to hospitalization. Fluorouracil dose was reduced by 75%. During the course of further chemotherapy the patient had no further toxicity. Genotyping revealed that this patient was homozygous for the TT genotype (DPYD*6/*6). Case report 2: A 69-year-old man was treated with mFLOX (oxaliplatin, leucovorin, 5-fluorouracil). After the first cycle the patient had grade 2 nausea and vomiting, loss of appetite, grade 2 diarrhea, deterioration of his general condition and a weight loss of 8 kg. The patient refused to continue with further chemotherapy due to toxicity. Genotyping revealed that this patient was heterozygous for the CT genotype (DPYD*1/*6) and heterozygous for the CG genotype at rs75017182 (DPYD HapB3). Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Genotype CT is associated with increased risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil in people with Colonic Neoplasms as compared to genotype CC.

More Details: Patients who carried the T allele (all patients were heterozygotes) had an increased risk of experiencing grade 3 or higher adverse events, both 5-fluorouracil-related adverse events and overall adverse events, as compared to those with the wild-type genotype. T allele is referred to in the paper as the DYPD*2A allele. Specifically those with the *2A allele had a greater incidence of nausea/vomiting (p=0.007) and neutropenia (p<0.001), as compared to those with the wild-type genotype. Those with the *2A allele were also significant less likely to complete all 12 treatment cycles as compared to wild-type patients. Additionally, one patient with both the DPYD*2A allele and the rs67376798 T allele had a grade 5 adverse event. Patients received FOLFOX (5-fluorouracil, leucovorin, oxaliplatin) or FOLFIRI (5-fluorouracil, leucovorin, irinotecan), alone or combined with cetuximab.

Association: Genotype CT is associated with increased risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to genotype CC.
More Details: Clinical data about adverse events were collected from patient records and laboratory charts for 12 weeks after the initiation of therapy. Delays or reductions in the administration of 5'FU or capecitabine due to adverse events were recorded as primary outcomes, and grade 3,4,5 adverse events were analyzed as secondary outcomes. Note: the reported parameters for this SNP are really for what the authors refer to as a "DPYD signature" that includes any minor alleles for the following SNPs in DPYD: rs3918290 (T), rs67376798 (A), rs75017182(C), rs56038477 (T).

Association: Allele G is associated with decreased response to capecitabine and fluorouracil in people with Neoplasm Metastasis as compared to allele A.

These SNPs were genotyped in a discovery cohort (N=62) and tested in a validation cohort (N=27). Both cohorts consisted of patients with colorectal cancer metastasis in liver. 36 SNPs were initially significantly associated with drug response but only 3 remained significant in the validation cohort (before Bonferroni correction): rs2291078 A, rs3772809 G, rs3772810 G. All three SNPs were in perfect LD, and were initially associated with the non-responder phenotype, but the association did not remain significant after Bonferroni correction.

Association: DPYD c.1129-5923C>G, c.1236G>A (HapB3) (assigned as deficiency phenotype) is associated with decreased risk of progression-free survival when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Breast Neoplasms, Colorectal Neoplasms, Stomach Neoplasms or Neoplasms as compared to DPYD non-deficient.
More Details:
"Subgroup analysis revealed
a consistently shorter PFS for c.1236G>A carriers. The trend
toward shorter PFS accompanied by a borderline P value in
the pooled DPYD variant carriers was, therefore, probably
largely driven by the survival outcomes of c.1236G>A, which
made up the majority of pooled DPYD variant carriers. For OS,
no significant difference was found in c.1236G>A carriers.
This discrepancy may be caused by differences in administration of other systemic treatment lines or other treatment modalities after fluoropyrimidine-based treatment
between all studied patient groups. Unfortunately, this information was not available."

One case of cardiac death was a patient with the CT genotype; all patients with the CC genotype (n=3) were in a subgroup of patients with "enhanced toxicity". In a replication cohort (Cohort 2, n=53) of cancer patients with adverse events following fluorouracil or capecitabine treatment, three C allele carriers developed severe toxicity. Analysis of Cohort 1 + Cohort 2 showed a dose-dependent association for this variant. When segregating by type of cancer, significant results were seen for those with gastroesophageal (p=0.007) and breast cancer (p=0.013) but not colorectal cancer. Please note alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

The gastric cancer patient was a 58-year-old male who received FOLFOX therapy and developed confused mentality and agitation on the third day of the third cycle, and was found to have hyperammonemic encephalopathy. The pancreatic cancer patient was a 54-year-old woman who received FOLFOX therapy and developed stupor during the 5th cycle, and was found to have hyperammonemic encephalopathy. The gastric cancer patient had the MTHFR GG (wild-type) genotype and the pancreatic cancer patient had the AA (variant) genotype. The gastric cancer patient also had the TYMS rs34489327 del/del genotype, the TYMS rs45445694 3G/3G genotype, the DPYD *9A/*5 diplotype, and the MTHFR rs1801131 GT genotype. The pancreatic cancer patient had the TYMS rs34489327 TTAAAG/del genotype, the TYMS rs45445694 3C/3C genotype, the DPYD *1/*5 diplotype, and the MTHFR rs1801131 TT (wild-type) genotype. The authors suggest that the encephalopathy may be related to the TYMS genotypes of the patients.

Association: Allele (CCGCGCCACTTGGCCTGCCTCCGTCCCG)2 is not associated with risk of Neutropenia or Leukopenia when treated with fluorouracil and leucovorin in people with Stomach Neoplasms as compared to allele (CCGCGCCACTTGGCCTGCCTCCGTCCCG)3.
More Details: Authors compared 28bp repeats and G>C in the second repeat of the 3R variant eg 2R/2R v 2R/3R v 3R/3R, 2R/2R, 2R/3C, 3C/3C, etc and no combinations were significant. rs34743033 maps to rs45445694

Association: Genotype GG is associated with decreased response to capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to genotypes AA + AG.
More Details: The authors examined disease free survival (DFS) as well as overall survival (OS). The GG genotype was associated with decreased DFS and OS.

Association: Allele T is associated with decreased activity of DPYD when assayed with fluorouracil as compared to allele C.
More Details: "Of the 25 deleterious variants, 9 exhibited DPD activities that were less than 15% of wildtype activity (K63E, R208Q, I281T, A549P, M680T, G795R, T737I, I948N, and H978Q)." G795R maps to rs758649719C>T in table S2.

Association: Genotype CT is associated with Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil in people with Neoplasms as compared to genotype CC.
More Details: This variant was found in combination with M166V (rs2297595) in two individuals of a group of 4 cases of fluorouracil toxicity but not together in controls (patients had leukopenia, neutropenia, alopecia and mucositis, enterotoxicity, respectively). Both patients had additional different variants in DPYD. Both patients had low/low normal DPYD activity (0.02 nmol/min/mg protein and 0.07 nmol/min/mg protein) compared to the range of normal activity values lying between 0.064 and 0.314nmol/min/mg protein.

Association: DPYD c.2846A>T + c.1129-5923C>G, c.1236G>A (HapB3) is associated with increased severity of Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil, FOLFIRINOX, FOLFOX or XELOX in people with Colorectal Neoplasms, Stomach Neoplasms, Gallbladder Neoplasms or Pancreatic Neoplasms.
More Details: "In total, 11 (7.5%) of the 146 patients had heterozygous DPYD mutations. The most common DPYD mutation was HappB3 (rs56038477), which was found in eight patients (72.7%). DPYD*2A (rs3918290) was observed in two patients (18.2%), and c.2846A>T (rs67376798) was found in one patient (9.1%). No mutations were found in c.1679T>G (rs55886062). Homozygous DPYD mutation carriers were not included in the fluoropyrimidine treatment group because of safety considerations; no such cases were observed in our screened cohort." "Severe toxicities (grade ≥3) were significantly more common in mutation carriers (72.7%, 8 of 11) than noncarriers (37.0%, 50 of 135; P = .03). "

Association: Genotype CT is not associated with risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil in people with Colorectal Neoplasms.
More Details: Note that there were only 5 heterozygotes in this population. Assessed within fluorouracil alone (n=404) and irinotecan and fluorouracil (IrFU, n=166) treatment groups. Primary toxicity outcome was delay and/or dose reduction due to toxicity within the first 12 weeks of treatment. Secondary outcome was any grade >=3 toxicity event reported within the first 12 weeks of treatment. In fluorouracil alone treatment: primary outcome, 27% of those with the CC genotype had dose-delay/reduction vs 50% of those with the CT genotype p=0.292, secondary outcome, 48% of those with the CC genotype had grade >=3 toxicity vs 50% of those with the CT genotype p=1.0. For IrFU: primary outcome, 38% of those with the CC genotype had dose-delay/reduction vs 67% of those with the CT genotype p=0.560, secondary outcome, 65% of those with the CC genotype had dose-delay/reduction vs 67% of those with the CT genotype p=1.0.

Association: Allele G is associated with decreased likelihood of Disease Progression when treated with epirubicin, fluorouracil and oxaliplatin in people with Stomach Neoplasms as compared to allele T.
More Details: Genotype GG also had a trend towards increased progression-free survival but it was not significant.

Association: Allele T is not associated with severity of Neutropenia when treated with fluorouracil, irinotecan and leucovorin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to allele C.
More Details: The study comprised overall more than 400 metastatic colorectal cancer (mCRC) patients treated with first-line FOLFIRI (folinic acid/leucovorin, fluorocil, irinotecan) grouped in a discovery and a replication cohorts. The allele was not associated with severe neutropenia (grade 3-4).

Association: Genotype TT is associated with increased severity of Diarrhea when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Neoplasms as compared to genotypes CC + CT.
More Details: this was not significant for incidence but for severity.

Association: GSTM1 null is associated with decreased response to anthracyclines and related substances, capecitabine, cyclophosphamide, fluorouracil and taxanes in women with Breast Neoplasms as compared to GSTM1 non-null.
Drugs: anthracyclines and related substances; capecitabine; cyclophosphamide; fluorouracil; taxanes

Association: Genotype AA is associated with risk of mucositis when treated with fluorouracil and radiotherapy in people with Rectal Neoplasms as compared to genotypes AT + TT.
More Details: The AA genotype showed increased risk in treatment arm 2 (PVI 5FU) but decreased risk in treatment arm 1 (bolus 5FU).

Association: Genotype CT is associated with Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil in people with Breast Neoplasms.
More Details: This variant was found in a single individual of a group of 4 cases of fluorouracil toxicity. Patient had mucositis, granulocytopenia and neurotoxicity. This individual had normal DPD activity (0.13 nmol/min/mg protein compared to the range of normal activity values lying between 0.064 and 0.314nmol/min/mg protein). Patient also receiving cyclophosphamide and methotrexate.

Association: Genotype CT is associated with Neutropenia when treated with fluorouracil in people with Colorectal Neoplasms.
More Details:
A single patient was as heterozygous for the *2A variant. The mutation
was found in the male who was one of four CRC patients suffering from grades 3-4 myelotoxicity (also described in methods as grades 3-4 neutropenia) upon 5-FU chemotherapy.

Association: Genotypes CC + CT is associated with increased overall survival when treated with epirubicin, fluorouracil and oxaliplatin in people with Stomach Neoplasms as compared to genotype TT.
More Details: This variant was also significantly associated with progression free survival.

Association: DPYD c.2846A>T + c.1129-5923C>G, c.1236G>A (HapB3) is not associated with decreased clinical benefit to fluorouracil, FOLFIRINOX, FOLFOX or XELOX in people with Colorectal Neoplasms, Stomach Neoplasms, Gallbladder Neoplasms or Pancreatic Neoplasms.
More Details: "In total, 11 (7.5%) of the 146 patients had heterozygous DPYD mutations. The most common DPYD mutation was HappB3 (rs56038477), which was found in eight patients (72.7%). DPYD*2A (rs3918290) was observed in two patients (18.2%), and c.2846A>T (rs67376798) was found in one patient (9.1%). No mutations were found in c.1679T>G (rs55886062). Homozygous DPYD mutation carriers were not included in the fluoropyrimidine treatment group because of safety considerations; no such cases were observed in our screened cohort." "The overall response rate (ORR) for DPYD mutation carriers was 72.7% (8 of 11), and for noncarriers, it was (47.4%; 64 of 135; P = .189, not statistically significant). Clinical benefit was observed in 81.8% of mutation carriers compared with 76.3% of nonmutation carriers (P = .45; Table 4; Fig 3)."

Association: Genotype GT is associated with Hyperammonemia when treated with fluorouracil in people with Pancreatic Neoplasms and Stomach Neoplasms.
More Details: 2 patients were studied, one with gastric cancer and one with pancreatic cancer. The gastric cancer patient was a 58-year-old male who received FOLFOX therapy and developed confused mentality and agitation on the third day of the third cycle, and was found to have hyperammonemic encephalopathy. The pancreatic cancer patient was a 54-year-old woman who received FOLFOX therapy and developed stupor during the 5th cycle, and was found to have hyperammonemic encephalopathy. The gastric cancer patient had the MTHFR GT genotype and the pancreatic cancer patient had the TT (wild-type) genotype. The gastric cancer patient also had the TYMS rs34489327 del/del genotype, the TYMS rs45445694 3G/3G genotype, the DPYD *9A/*5 diplotype, and the MTHFR rs1801133 GG (wild-type) genotype.The pancreatic cancer patient had the TYMS rs34489327 TTAAAG/del genotype, the TYMS rs45445694 3C/3C genotype, the DPYD *1/*5 diplotype, and the MTHFR rs1801133 AA genotype. The authors suggest that the encephalopathy may be related to the TYMS genotypes of the patients.

Association: Allele T is not associated with response to cisplatin and fluorouracil in men with Head and Neck Neoplasms as compared to allele C.
More Details: No significant difference in the frequency of the DPYD*4 allele (T) was found between nonresponders and responders to treatment. Responders were those with a complete or partial response.

Association: Genotype AG is associated with increased severity of Anemia when treated with fluorouracil in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to genotype AA.
More Details: "DPYD 85T>C showed a trend toward increased anemia, particularly during the second chemotherapy cycle. Although the difference was not significant, this suggests a possible cumulative effect of the variant on drug metabolism over time. " "For the DPYD 85T>C polymorphism, the majority of individuals were homozygous wild-type (72.9%), while 27.1% were heterozygous; no homozygous variant carriers were observed." Alleles complemented.

Association: Genotype CT is associated with risk of Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil in people with Gastrointestinal Neoplasms.
More Details: Within a cohort of 45 patients with gastrointestinal cancer who had developed 5-fluorouracil toxicity, 2 patients were identified with the DPYD*2A allele. One was a 64-year-old male with colorectal adenocarcinoma with grade 4 mucositis and neutropenia, and one was a 68-year-old female with esophageal squamous cell carcinoma with grade 4 mucositis.

Association: Allele T is not associated with severity of Neutropenia when treated with fluorouracil, irinotecan and leucovorin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to allele C.
More Details: The study comprised overall more than 400 metastatic colorectal cancer (mCRC) patients treated with first-line FOLFIRI (folinic acid/leucovorin, fluorocil, irinotecan) grouped in a discovery and a replication cohorts. The allele was not associated with severe neutropenia (grade 3-4).

Association: Allele A is not associated with severity of Neutropenia when treated with fluorouracil, irinotecan and leucovorin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to allele G.
More Details: The study comprised overall more than 400 metastatic colorectal cancer (mCRC) patients treated with first-line FOLFIRI (folinic acid/leucovorin, fluorocil, irinotecan) grouped in a discovery and a replication cohorts. The allele was not associated with severe neutropenia (grade 3-4).

Association: DPYD deficiency is not associated with decreased risk of overall survival or progression-free survival when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Breast Neoplasms, Colorectal Neoplasms, Stomach Neoplasms or Neoplasms as compared to DPYD non-deficient.
More Details:
"The results of our study showed no significant differences in
PFS and OS between the pooled DPYD variant carriers and
matched DPYD wild-type patients, suggesting that DPYDguided dose individualization can likely be performed safely without compromising effectiveness.""Exploratory analysis was performed for
all variants pooled and for the individual genetic subgroups
DPYD*2A, c.2846A>T, and c.1236G>A. "

Association: Genotype CT is associated with increased severity of Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to genotype CC.
More Details: Out of 8 patients who had grade 3-4 toxicity, one was heterozygous for this variant. Patient had grade 4 neutropenia, grade 3 fever, grade 2 anemia, diarrhea and infection.

Association: Allele C is associated with increased likelihood of Disease Progression when treated with epirubicin, fluorouracil and oxaliplatin in people with Stomach Neoplasms as compared to allele T.
More Details: Genotype TT also had a trend towards increased progression-free survival but it was not significant.

Association: CYP3A4 *1 + *3 are not associated with Drug Toxicity when treated with fluorouracil, irinotecan or oxaliplatin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms.
More Details: Patients were taking either IFL (irinotecan + fluorouracil + leucovorin; n=114), FOLFOX (fluorouracil + oxaliplatin + leucovorin; n=299) or IROX (irinotecan + oxaliplatin; n=107). No significant associations were seen between these variants and grade >=3 diarrhea, vomiting, paresthesias or febrile neutropenia or grade >=4 neutropenia in any of the treatment groups OR all treatment groups considered together. Significance level was set at 0.01.

Association: Genotype CC is not associated with increased likelihood of Drug Toxicity when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Neoplasms as compared to genotype TT.
More Details: Alleles complemented to plus chromosomal strand. "In the overall sample (DPYD wildtype and variant carriers), patients with one variant allele of miR-27a rs895819 were at an increased risk of fluoropyrimidine-associated toxicity during their total treatment period (OR (95% CI) = 2.38 (1.26–4.57)), while patients with two variant alleles were not at an increased risk (OR (95%) = 1.49 (0.63–3.43))"

Association: Allele A is not associated with decreased activity of DPYD when assayed with fluorouracil as compared to allele G.
More Details: "The activities of the remaining 44 variants were greater than 70% of wildtype DPD activity, and these variants were, therefore, considered neutral even if p-values were less than 0.05." S326F maps to rs575763449G>A in table S1.

Association: Allele C is associated with increased discontinuation of capecitabine, fluorouracil or tegafur in people with Neoplasms as compared to allele G.
More Details: Discontinuation due to severe toxicity: gastrointestinal, hematological, and other treatment-related hospitalization.

Association: CYP3A4 *1 + *3 are not associated with response to fluorouracil, irinotecan or oxaliplatin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms.
More Details: Patients were taking either IFL (irinotecan + fluorouracil + leucovorin; n=114), FOLFOX (fluorouracil + oxaliplatin + leucovorin; n=299) or IROX (irinotecan + oxaliplatin; n=107). No significant associations were seen between these *1B (−329 A>G) and *3 (1334 T>C)  and confirmed response rate, overall survival or time to progression in any of the treatment groups OR all treatment groups considered together. Significance level was set at 0.01.

Association: Allele T is not associated with decreased activity of DPYD when assayed with fluorouracil as compared to allele C.
More Details: "The activities of the remaining 44 variants were greater than 70% of wildtype DPD activity, and these variants were, therefore, considered neutral even if p-values were less than 0.05." R357H maps to rs377143350C>T in table S1.

Association: Genotype AT is associated with increased risk of Diarrhea when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Gastrointestinal Neoplasms.
More Details: Two patients who carried the A allele received full-dose chemotherapy and developed grade 3-4 diarrhea. Please refer to the paper for a full breakdown of the different grade 1-2/grade 3-4 toxicities that some these patients developed.

Association: Genotype GG is not associated with response to fluorouracil, irinotecan or oxaliplatin in people with Colorectal Neoplasms as compared to genotypes AA + AG.
More Details: Patients were taking either IFL (irinotecan + fluorouracil + leucovorin; n=114), FOLFOX (fluorouracil + oxaliplatin + leucovorin; n=299) or IROX (irinotecan + oxaliplatin; n=107). No significant association was seen between this variant and confirmed response rate, overall survival or time to progression in any of the treatment groups OR all treatment groups considered together. Significance level was set at 0.01. Please note that alleles have been complemented to the plus chromosomal strand.

Association: Allele T is not associated with response to fluorouracil, leucovorin and oxaliplatin in people with Esophageal Neoplasms and Stomach Neoplasms as compared to allele G.
Genotype TT is associated with increased risk of Drug Toxicity and Death when treated with fluorouracil in women with Rectal Neoplasms.
More Details: Case report. The patient was a 44-year-old female who was administered 5-fluorouracil (900mg) and leucovorin (200mg) via i.v. on a weekly basis. Five days after the first treatment, the patient developed stomatitis. Five days after the second injection, the patient developed fever, severe stomatitis, leucopenia and thrombocytopenia. The next day she developed severe pancytopenia and received a transfusion. The patient died 8 days later due to infectious complications. Patient was found to have complete DPD deficiency. Sequence analysis showed homozygosity for the *2A allele (IVS14+1G>A).

Association: Allele C is associated with increased severity of Drug Toxicity and Treatment modification when treated with capecitabine or fluorouracil in people with Neoplasms as compared to allele T.
Biogeographical Groups: Multiple groups, 89% white, 5% Black/African, 2% Asian 19 (2%), 4%Other, 1% Hispanic or Latino
More Details: "systemic FPs (5-fluorouracil or capecitabine) therapy for any cancer type for at least two cycles". "In the primary analysis of 799 patients without a validated variant, carriers of any of the five uncommon suspected deleterious variants (n = 9; 1.1% of patients) had significantly higher risk of the primary toxicity endpoint than non-carriers (67% vs. 24%; adjOR 7.36; 95% CI 1.7538.20; p = 0.009)."
